_comment: OPTIONAL operator overrides for FHIR Plan-Net payers. The validated public endpoints (app/planet_registry.py) are already wired as VERIFIED filters out of the box — you do NOT need to list them here. Use this file only to add a payer that isn't in the registry (e.g. one needing a free developer key) or to override a registry entry. Copy to payers.json. Each entry maps to a plan id grouped under `category`; `payer` defaults to `id`; add `api_key_header`/`api_key` if the payer requires a key; `states` scopes a regional payer so it's only queried for in-state providers. IMPORTANT: only wire an endpoint that passes the per-NPI round-trip (see `python -m app.verify_payers` and docs/provenance.md) — a directory that ignores the NPI filter fabricates 'in-network', and one that returns nothing for listed providers fabricates 'not in-network'. Payers you do NOT wire still appear as clearly-labeled ESTIMATED filters from the curated catalog (app/catalog.py).

payers._note: EXAMPLE shape. Priority Partners (MD Medicaid, Johns Hopkins) and Advantage MD (MD Medicare Advantage) are ALREADY wired automatically from the validated registry — listing them here is redundant. Replace this with a payer you want to add yourself, e.g. one requiring api_key_header/api_key.
payers.label: Example Regional Payer

payers._note: EXAMPLE OAuth 2.0 client-credentials shape, for nationals that gate their public directory behind app registration (e.g. Aetna). Provide the FHIR base_url + token_url from the dev portal; keep the secrets OUT of this file by naming env vars (client_id_env/client_secret_env) and setting them in the deploy environment. Use lookup_mode 'two_step' when PractitionerRole can't be searched by practitioner.identifier but Practitioner can be searched by NPI (Aetna's case). Verify with `python -m app.verify_payers` before trusting it.
payers.label: Aetna
